title: stat -json does not output valid JSON when using libcache
body:
### Version

Yosys 0.53+3 (git sha1 6378ba10e)

### On which OS did this happen?

Linux

### Reproduction Steps

We `tee stat -liberty ex.lib -json` to a JSON file and then ingest it into other tools. When using the recently added `libcache -enable -all`, this command no longer outputs valid JSON because of these logs: https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/blob/6378ba10eb9263dce58fb70afd1b22a62900e273/passes/techmap/libparse.cc#L44

https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/blob/6378ba10eb9263dce58fb70afd1b22a62900e273/passes/techmap/libparse.cc#L54

Repro steps:

`yosys libparse.tcl`

libparse.tcl:
```
yosys libcache -enable -all
yosys read_verilog tests/liberty/small.v;
yosys synth -top small;
yosys dfflibmap -info -liberty tests/liberty/normal.lib
puts "stats json:"
yosys stat -liberty tests/liberty/normal.lib -json
```

### Expected Behavior

The output of the `stat -liberty tests/liberty/normal.lib -json` command should produce valid JSON that can be directly exported to a file.

### Actual Behavior

The added logs are triggered in the LibertyParser logic invoked by the stat pass.

There are a few ways to fix this, such as removing the logs altogether or passing some quiet flag through.

@jix how do you think it is best to resolve this?
